summary: Aggregate item/game-mode cleanup PR targeting high-fuzzy-match low-hanging fruit. The diff updates item collision and item_266F helpers, a Fox Blaster item routine, and gm_1601 signatures/control-flow/local cleanup. The automated report says matched code improved by 3040 bytes, with 16 new 100% matches and 7 additional fuzzy improvements.

key_files.role: Primary item cleanup and matching work: rewrote binary-search-like helper logic, changed signatures and casts, added small wrapper functions, adjusted PAD_STACK/local variable shapes, and scoped a dont_inline pragma around it_8026E7E0.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev bot report lists multiple it_266F new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Header prototype updates for recovered/changed item helper signatures and declarations for newly added wrapper helpers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: it_8026C65C return changed u8 to int
raw_diff.diff: it_8026EA20 arg4 changed UNK_T* to int*
raw_diff.diff: declarations added for it_8026E15C_inline1, it_8026E15C_inline2, it_8026E248_inline, it_8026E32C_inline

key_files.role: Item collision cleanup: removed large disabled alternative blocks and replaced copied hitbox checks with an ARRAY_SIZE loop; adjusted GET_ITEM use and left/right bounding box swap logic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: it_8026FCF8 removed #if 0 alternatives and introduced for loop over ARRAY_SIZE(item->x5D4_hitboxes)
raw_diff.diff: it_80271A58 switched item_gobj->user_data to GET_ITEM(item_gobj) and changed left/right negation order
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists it_8026FC00, it_8026FCF8, and it_80271A58 as new 100% matches

key_files.role: Fox Blaster item routine fuzzy-match adjustment using a temporary zero local, with an in-diff TODO warning it may be a fake improvement versus an idiomatic for loop.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: it_802ADF10 added TODO about probable fake improvement and new_var2
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists it_802ADF10 improvement from 96.22% to 98.15%

key_files.role: Game-mode cleanup: added gm/types.h include, renamed locals, adjusted pointer math, switch/control-flow, bool compare, signature body for fn_80169574, and stack/local shaping in gm_801674C4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: gm_80163298 local renames and pointer-math TODO
raw_diff.diff: gm_80167470 changed if/return shape to switch case
raw_diff.diff: fn_80169574 signature changed void* buf to s8* buf
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report lists gm_80167470, fn_80169574, and fn_8016A488 as new 100% matches

key_files.role: Header signature update matching fn_80169574 body change.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: fn_80169574 prototype changed from void* buf to s8* buf

what_changed: PR body frames the work as an aggregate PR for multiple files, ideally matching high-fuzzy low-hanging-fruit item files.
Automated report records matched code at 54.29%, +0.08%, +3040 bytes, with 16 new matches.
New 100% matches listed by the bot include it_8026F6BC, it_8026E7E0, it_8026C65C, it_80271A58, it_8026FCF8, it_8026FC00, gm_80167470, fn_80169574, and fn_8016A488.
itcoll.c removed large #if 0 alternate implementations in it_8026FCF8 and replaced repeated hitbox checks with a for loop over ARRAY_SIZE(item->x5D4_hitboxes).

it_266F.c introduced several matching-oriented local variable and stack changes, wrapper functions around direct calls, a scoped dont_inline pragma around it_8026E7E0, and signature changes for it_8026C65C and it_8026EA20.
gm_1601.c updated local naming in gm_80163298, preserved a pointer-math TODO, changed gm_80167470 control flow to a switch shape, and typed fn_80169574's buffer as s8*.
itfoxblaster.c added a temporary zero local in it_802ADF10 and an explicit TODO that the change may be a fake improvement because the idiomatic for-loop shape was not yet matching.

smart_moves.title: Converted repeated hitbox-copy checks into a bounded ARRAY_SIZE loop
smart_moves.details: In it_8026FCF8, copied generated-looking checks over four hitboxes were replaced with a for loop using ARRAY_SIZE(item->x5D4_hitboxes), while the bot report shows it_8026FCF8 reached 100%.

smart_moves.title: Updated headers when body signatures changed
smart_moves.details: fn_80169574 changed from void* to s8* in both gm_1601.c and gm_1601.h; it_8026C65C and it_8026EA20 signature changes were mirrored in it_266F.h.

smart_moves.title: Left uncertainty in source comments instead of pretending a cleanup was final
smart_moves.details: The diff explicitly marks remaining pointer math in gm_80163298 and the Fox Blaster temporary variable as potential TODO/fake-improvement areas.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: gm_80163298 POTENTIAL TODO pointer math comment
raw_diff.diff: it_802ADF10 TODO about fake improvement and idiomatic for loop

decomp_lessons.lesson: A natural fixed-size loop can match where copied generated-looking blocks existed, as shown by it_8026FCF8 replacing manual hitbox checks with a for loop over ARRAY_SIZE and reaching 100% in the bot report.
When a recovered body proves a more specific pointer type, update both implementation and header; fn_80169574 uses s8* buf so indexed writes no longer need ((s8*) buf)[size].
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: fn_80169574 body and prototype change

decomp_lessons.lesson: Matching-oriented local lifetime/register-shape changes can improve scores, but PR evidence here labels some of them as unfinished or possibly fake, so they should not be generalized without verifier evidence.

naming_conventions.name: Clear local role names over v/temp numbering when role is evident
naming_conventions.details: gm_80163298 renames v1/v2/v3/v4/v5 to score/index/record/save_data/offset, making the data roles clearer while retaining a TODO for unresolved pointer math.

naming_conventions.name: Use short subsystem object locals when role is clear
naming_conventions.details: it_8026E_inline uses Item* ip from GET_ITEM and CollData* coll for item/collision roles.

naming_conventions.name: Avoid treating placeholder names as accepted style
naming_conventions.details: The diff introduces placeholder names such as new_var, new_val, and new_var2 for matching; PR evidence does not support promoting these as naming conventions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: multiple it_266F.c hunks with new_var/new_val

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Several functions changed PAD_STACK amounts, including it_8026E_inline, it_8026E32C, it_8026EA20, gm_801674C4, and it_8026FCF8. Evidence shows score/match improvements, but not a detailed objdiff rationale for each stack pad.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: PAD_STACK changes in gm_801674C4, it_8026E_inline, it_8026E32C, it_8026EA20, it_8026FCF8

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: it_8026E7E0 is wrapped in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop and is reported as a new 100% match. This is evidence for the local tactic, not a global endorsement of pragmas.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Wrapper helpers to affect call/inlining shape
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Small wrappers it_8026E15C_inline1, it_8026E15C_inline2, it_8026E248_inline, and it_8026E32C_inline were added around existing item collision helpers and declared in the header.

text_corpus.jsonl: it_8026E15C, it_8026E248, it_8026E32C improved but not all reached 100%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: gm_80167470 changed from an if/return pattern to a switch-case shape and was reported as a new 100% match.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Pointer arithmetic retained with explicit TODO

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, changed files, line counts
human_pr_text.md: author stated aggregate matching/cleanup intent
notes: Changed files, major code edits, and automated match deltas are well supported.
There are no human review comments in the slice, so reviewer intent and acceptance rationale cannot be inferred.
The bot report supports match/improvement outcomes at symbol level, but the slice does not include per-function objdiff details or local build logs.
Several edits are explicitly matching tactics or possible fake improvements; they should not be promoted as general style without curator review.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Fixed-size repeated hitbox checks may be recoverable as ARRAY_SIZE loops
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2196, it_8026FCF8 replaced repeated manual checks of item hitboxes with `for (i = 0; i < ARRAY_SIZE(item->x5D4_hitboxes); i++)` and the automated report shows the function reached 100%. This supports trying a natural fixed-size loop before preserving copied generated blocks.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Specific recovered pointer types can remove casts and should be mirrored in headers
accepted_candidate_records.text: fn_80169574 changed from `void* buf` plus `((s8*) buf)[size]` to `s8* buf` plus `buf[size]`, and the gm_1601.h prototype was updated in the same PR. The bot report lists fn_80169574 as a new 100% match.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR #2196 as a cautionary example for matching tactics that need narrow evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2196 contains local matching tactics including PAD_STACK adjustments, wrapper helpers, placeholder locals such as new_var/new_var2, and a scoped dont_inline pragma. Some symbols reached 100%, but one source comment explicitly says a Fox Blaster change is probably a fake improvement and that the idiomatic for loop did not yet match. This PR is suitable as an example for requiring narrow objdiff/build evidence and not generalizing tactics as style.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards already warn about pragmas and matching tactics; this PR provides a concrete historical example for curator review, not a new accepted standard.

source_update_candidates.title: itcoll.c includes item hitbox collision-copy helpers matched by natural loops
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2196, src/melee/it/itcoll.c function it_8026FCF8 scans item hitboxes and copies a matching HitCapsule via lbColl_CopyHitCapsule; the PR changed this to an ARRAY_SIZE loop and the bot reported a 100% match.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful path-level search fact; should be curated against current source before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not treat placeholder locals named new_var/new_val/new_var2 as a naming convention; they appear to be matching artifacts.
Do not infer that #pragma dont_inline is broadly acceptable; this PR only shows a scoped local use with an automated match report.

Do not claim all changed functions fully matched; the bot report lists several as improvements below 100%, including it_8026E15C, it_8026E248, it_8026E32C, gm_801674C4, it_802ADF10, it_8026C530, and gm_80162A98.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are present in the loaded evidence.
